rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-building section for H2, not just in a background list. The citing article explicitly relies on Boyd, Chandy, and Cunha (2010) to support claims that CMOs have distinctive marketing information advantages and boundary-spanning capabilities. Those claims are then used to justify the mechanism that CMO presence dampens the positive effect of value-appropriation emphasis on choosing acquisitions over alliances.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Boyd et al. (2010) as conceptual grounding for the strategic role of CMOs, then uses that logic to develop and test a new moderation hypothesis in governance mode choice. In this sense, the cited work provides prior CMO-role foundations that support the citing paper's boundary-condition argument.
